footer.tagline: SpeakWith: your spoken ideas, captured as a daily document.

hero.headline: Speak Now. Refine Later.
hero.lead: Speak your day into a clean markdown file on Mac or iOS. Read it, edit it, share it. It works on its own, and it opens in VS Code, Obsidian, or any tool you point at it.
hero.note: Works on Mac and iOS. Your voice never leaves your device. No account required.

moments.title: Say it now. Use it later.
moments.heroAlt: SpeakWith for Mac in live dictation: spoken words appearing as transcribed lines beneath a live waveform.
moments.heroCaption: Speak, and watch the thought land as text before it slips away.

platforms.title: A recorder for the desk, a companion for everywhere else
platforms.sub: SpeakWith is a voice recorder. It is tuned for speech and dictation, the way a person actually talks, not for capturing music or ambient sound.

items.name: SpeakWith for Mac
items.body: Live and batch recorders, audio and video import, and a choice of transcription engines: Apple on-device, Whisper, or Parakeet. Every recording lands in your daily document, with playback you can click straight into.

items.name: SpeakWith for iOS
items.body: A voice recorder for when you are away from the desk. Live dictation, a dictation board that collects what you capture, and your transcripts and audio files kept close. Transcription runs on-device with Apple Speech.
points: English today, more languages in development

moat.title: Your file. Your machine. Your AI.

items.label: Local.
items.body: Your recordings never leave your machine. Period.

items.label: Markdown.
items.body: A plain .md file you own. Open it in VS Code, Obsidian, or any editor. Feed it to any tool.

items.label: No account.
items.body: No login. No subscription cloud. No data trade.

items.label: On your computer.
items.body: Transcription and indexing run on your Mac. Nothing is uploaded. Ever.

inclusive.heading: You don't have to talk to AI to use this.
inclusive.p1: SpeakWith works on its own. Speak, get a clean markdown file, then edit it, search its text, and share it. Keep it that way for as long as you like.
inclusive.p2Html: The file is also something you can hand to an AI when you decide that's useful. AI is a thing the file can do, not a thing you have to do.

items.title: A document for every day
items.body: Everything you record or type on a given day flows into a single organised document, in the order it happened. Tomorrow, a fresh one begins.

items.title: Bring in audio & video
items.body: Drop in a voice memo, a recorded call, or a screen recording. SpeakWith writes it up and folds it into your daily document like anything else.

items.title: Speak and keep working
items.body: Speaking and working no longer take turns. Your dictation flows into the document on its own, so you can organise sections, paste in snippets, or type something completely different at the same time, without interrupting the words you're capturing.

items.title: Speaking rhythm, on screen
items.body: A live meter shows the pauses inside what you are saying. Over time, that quiet feedback shapes how you speak: you hold a topic together longer, and the text on the other end gets easier to work with.

parallel.heading: Like having an extra pair of hands
parallel.p1Html: Most tools force you to shift your focus from thinking to operating an application. SpeakWith removes that friction. Every recorder lives in the same application, and what you capture writes out to the project you have selected.
parallel.p2: You can control SpeakWith with a remote microphone, so you never need to touch the keyboard, and there is no key to remember before you start recording. Your hands stay on the work while your voice does the writing.

rhythm.heading: The longer you use it, the better you speak.
rhythm.p1: While you record, a small meter shows the pauses inside what you are saying. Over weeks, the effect adds up. You notice when a thought starts to fragment, you stay on a topic a beat longer, and the next time you do it a little more cleanly.
rhythm.p2: The text on the other end changes too. Single topics arrive in coherent pieces, and paragraphs hold together, so your daily document is something you can hand to a person or a tool without reshaping it first.

homebase.eyebrow: Built for how real work happens
homebase.heading: The home base your thinking returns to
homebase.p1: Real work runs in parallel. You start something that takes time to come back: a long process, a tool working away, a task you've handed off. Instead of waiting, you begin the next thing, then another. Before long, several are in flight at once.
homebase.p2: Tools come and go; your daily document stays. It is the one place that holds every thread in a single file that is yours: what each one is for, where you left it, what to check when it lands. You can keep many things moving at once because you are not holding them all in your head.

items.q: Does SpeakWith need an internet connection?
items.a: No. Recording, transcription, organising your daily document, and searching its text all happen on your Mac. SpeakWith does its core work offline.

items.q: Where are my recordings and documents stored?
items.a: As ordinary files on your Mac. You can open, copy, back up, and delete them with any tool you like. They are not locked inside the app or held in an account.

Automatic transcription is very good but never perfect. Names and specialist terms are the usual places small errors appear. Your daily document is fully editable, so treat the transcript as an excellent first draft you can correct.

items.q: Does using SpeakWith change how I speak?
items.a: Many people find that it does. While you record, a small meter shows the pauses inside what you are saying, a quiet form of feedback that you can glance at without breaking your train of thought. Over weeks, you start to hold a topic together longer and let it land in a single, coherent piece. Your daily document gets easier to work with, and so does the way you speak to people away from the screen.

items.q: Can I import audio and video I already have?
items.a: Yes. Import a voice memo, a recorded meeting, or a screen recording, and SpeakWith writes it up the same way it writes up a live recording.

items.q: Can I keep working while a recording is written up?
items.a: Yes. That is one of SpeakWith's best features. Type and paste into the day's document while dictation is still processing; everything ends up in the correct chronological order.

It's on the way, and it will be free. SpeakWith for iOS is a companion voice recorder: live dictation, a dictation board that collects what you capture, and your transcripts and audio files on hand. It transcribes on-device with Apple Speech, and it works alongside the Mac app rather than replacing it.

items.q: On iOS, the permission prompt says speech is "sent to Apple". Is my voice uploaded?
items.a: No. That line is Apple's standard wording for the speech-recognition permission, shown to every app that asks for it. SpeakWith transcribes on-device using Apple's built-in dictation: your audio is turned into text on your iPhone or iPad. The only thing that downloads is the speech model itself, once. After that, transcription runs on your device.
English today. Support for more languages is in development, and they will arrive in updates as they are ready.

It is a voice recorder. SpeakWith is tuned for speech and dictation, the way a person actually talks, so it does its best work on the human voice. It is not built for capturing music or ambient sound.

download.heading: Get SpeakWith
download.body: SpeakWith runs on Mac and iOS — it needs a Mac with Apple Silicon (M1 or later) running macOS 26 (Tahoe) or later, or an iPhone on iOS 26 or later. Download it, grant microphone access, and your first recording is a click away.

header.heading: Choose how SpeakWith works for you
header.sub: Start with built-in dictation, then upgrade for advanced engines, batch transcription, and smart formatting.

liveDictation.subtitle: Speak and watch your words appear instantly

batch.subtitle: Record now, get polished text after

formatting.subtitle: Automatically groups speech into clean paragraphs

escalation.subtitle: Detects low-confidence audio and re-runs it on a higher-accuracy model

engines.heading: Three engines, one app
engines.sub: SpeakWith picks the right tool for the job. Here's what each engine is great at.

items.name: Apple
items.body: Built into macOS. Instant, offline, and free. Perfect for quick dictation and everyday notes.

items.name: Whisper
items.body: Maximum robustness on tough audio: accents, background noise, crosstalk. Steps up from a fast model to a high-accuracy model when it senses lower confidence.
items.note: Automatic escalation requires the optional accuracy add-on.

items.name: Parakeet
items.body: The speed champion. Near-instant transcription with excellent accuracy on clean audio.

channels.heading: Two ways to get SpeakWith
channels.sub: Same app, two storefronts. Pick what fits how you work.

items.heading: Coming soon to the App Store
items.body: A free version, fully sandboxed to App Store standards, is coming to the App Store. Pro features (advanced engines, import, batch, and smart formatting) will follow as a subscription.

items.heading: 14-day free trial, then buy once
items.body: Try the full app free for 14 days. After that it unlocks with a single one-time purchase, no subscription. The direct build is the complete, unsandboxed version of SpeakWith.

pricing.finePrint: The App Store version will be a free, complete product on its own, with no purchase required to dictate, and is coming soon. Pro features (advanced engines, audio and video import, batch transcription, and smart formatting) will unlock through an App Store subscription (also coming soon) or by buying once direct on Gumroad, available today. The Gumroad build includes a 14-day free trial that locks after 14 days until you buy. The iOS companion app will be free.

automation.heading: Automation
automation.intro: SpeakWith has two recorders you can drive from outside the app: Live dictation (real-time, finalised at each pause) and Live audio (voice-activated). You can start, stop, and control either one from the command line, a macOS Shortcut, Automator, or a hotkey launcher — with no extra setup. There are two equivalent ways to send a command: the speakwith:// URL scheme (simplest) and AppleScript (for scripting environments that prefer it).

paragraphs.text:
The fastest way to begin a take is the URL scheme — one line any shell can run. Start the Live dictation recorder:

open "speakwith://live-dictation/start"

Prefer AppleScript? This does the same thing:

osascript -e 'tell application "SpeakWith" to start recording recorder live dictation'

Start the Live audio (voice-activated) recorder instead:

open "speakwith://batch/start"

Stop the current take (the microphone stays powered on, ready to start again):

open "speakwith://live-dictation/stop"

Each recorder supports five actions. The table below shows the Live dictation recorder. For the Live audio recorder, replace “live dictation” with “batch” in the AppleScript command, and “live-dictation” with “batch” in the URL.

ActionAppleScript commandURL scheme
Power on (microphone only)power on recorder live dictationspeakwith://live-dictation/power-on
Power off (release the microphone)power off recorder live dictationspeakwith://live-dictation/power-off
Start a takestart recording recorder live dictationspeakwith://live-dictation/start
Stop a takestop recording recorder live dictationspeakwith://live-dictation/stop
Set standby on / offset standby recorder live dictation enabled true (or false)speakwith://live-dictation/standby?on=true (or false)
Each AppleScript command runs inside tell application "SpeakWith" to … — for example, osascript -e 'tell application "SpeakWith" to power off recorder batch'.

A few things worth knowing: every command is safe to repeat — running “start” while already recording, or “power on” while already on, simply does nothing. Standby only affects the recorder that is currently powered on; it never powers a recorder on by itself.
By default these commands find SpeakWith by name. If you have more than one SpeakWith build installed, pin the exact one you want by its bundle identifier:
osascript -e 'tell application id "com.ikisystems.speakwith.macos.gumroad" to start recording recorder live dictation'

The shipping bundle identifiers are com.ikisystems.speakwith.macos.gumroad (Gumroad), com.ikisystems.speakwith.macos.appstore (Mac App Store), and com.ikisystems.speakwith.macos (Developer ID direct download).

Because these are ordinary commands and URLs, you can turn any of them into a keyboard shortcut today. Drop one into a macOS Shortcut, an Automator “Run Shell Script” action, or a launcher such as Raycast, Alfred, or Keyboard Maestro, and give it the hotkey you like.

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: SpeakWith is built around a simple principle: your words are yours. This policy explains what SpeakWith does and does not do with the recordings, transcripts, and documents you create.

sections.heading: Your recordings are processed on your device

paragraphs.text: SpeakWith records, transcribes, organises, and searches entirely on your own device, whether that is your Mac or your iOS device. Turning speech into text, shaping it into your daily document, and answering your searches all happen locally. SpeakWith does not need an internet connection to do its core work, and it does not send your recordings or transcripts to a server to be processed.

SpeakWith does not include usage analytics, advertising, or behavioural tracking. We do not collect a record of how you use the app, what you record, or what you search for. There are no third-party trackers in the application.

Everything SpeakWith produces (your recordings, transcripts, and daily documents) is stored as ordinary files on your device. You can open, copy, move, back up, and delete them with any tool you like. They are not locked inside a proprietary store, and they are not held in an account that we control.

If something in SpeakWith goes wrong, a diagnostic report can help us fix it. SpeakWith never sends such a report automatically. Crash and diagnostic reporting is strictly opt-in: nothing leaves your device unless you explicitly choose to send it, and you can decline or turn it off at any time.

This website is a static information site. It does not set tracking cookies and does not profile its visitors. Standard server request logs may exist at the hosting provider, as they do for any website.
If this policy changes, the updated version will be posted here with a new date. Material changes will be summarised at the top of the page.
Questions about privacy? Reach us through the support channels listed with the app.

terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of the SpeakWith application. By installing or using SpeakWith, you agree to them. Please read them carefully.

paragraphs.text: SpeakWith grants you a personal, non-exclusive, non-transferable licence to install and use the application on Mac computers and iOS devices you own or control, in accordance with these terms. You may not redistribute, resell, rent, or sublicense the application, or attempt to reverse engineer it except where that restriction is prohibited by law.

SpeakWith converts speech to text using automated processing. Automated transcription is a powerful aid, but it is not guaranteed to be accurate or complete. Results depend on factors including recording quality, background noise, accents, specialist vocabulary, and the nature of what was said.

You should treat transcripts as a high-quality first draft to review and correct, not as a verbatim or certified record. Do not rely on SpeakWith's output for any purpose where an error could cause harm, loss, or legal consequence without independently verifying it.

You own the recordings, transcripts, and documents you create with SpeakWith. SpeakWith does not claim any rights over your content. You are responsible for what you record and for having the right to record it. Because your content is stored as files on your own device, you are also responsible for backing it up.

SpeakWith is provided "as is" and "as available", without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, including but not limited to warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement. We do not warrant that the application will be uninterrupted, error-free, or that every recording will be processed successfully.

To the maximum extent permitted by law, SpeakWith and IKI Systems will not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages, or for any loss of data, arising out of or related to your use of the application. Nothing in these terms excludes liability that cannot be excluded under applicable law.
These terms may be updated from time to time. The current version will always be posted here with its date. Continued use of SpeakWith after a change means you accept the updated terms.
Questions about these terms can be directed to the support channels listed with the application.
